briefdescription.para: Current driver version.

detaileddescription.para: This software is licensed under terms that can be found in the LICENSE file in the root directory of this software component. If no LICENSE file comes with this software, it is provided AS-IS.

briefdescription.para: Default I2C address of VL53L8CX sensor. Can be changed using function vl53l8cx_set_i2c_address() function is called.
Macro VL53L8CX_RESOLUTION_4X4 or VL53L8CX_RESOLUTION_8X8 allows setting sensor in 4x4 mode or 8x8 mode, using function vl53l8cx_set_resolution().
Macro VL53L8CX_TARGET_ORDER_STRONGEST or VL53L8CX_TARGET_ORDER_CLOSEST are used to select the target order for data output.

Macro VL53L8CX_RANGING_MODE_CONTINUOUS and VL53L8CX_RANGING_MODE_AUTONOMOUS are used to change the ranging mode. Autonomous mode can be used to set a precise integration time, whereas continuous is always maximum.
The default power mode is VL53L8CX_POWER_MODE_WAKEUP. User can choose two different modes to save power consumption when is the device is not used:

listitem.para: VL53L8CX_POWER_MODE_SLEEP: This mode retains the firmware and the configuration. It is recommended when the device needs to quickly wake-up.

VL53L8CX_POWER_MODE_DEEP_SLEEP: This mode clears all memory, by consequence the firmware, the configuration and the calibration are lost. It is recommended when the device sleeps during a long time as it consumes a very low current consumption. Both modes can be changed using function vl53l8cx_set_power_mode().

briefdescription.para: Macro VL53L8CX_STATUS_OK indicates that VL53L5 sensor has no error. Macro VL53L8CX_STATUS_ERROR indicates that something is wrong (value, I2C access, ...). Macro VL53L8CX_MCU_ERROR is used to indicate a MCU issue.
Definitions for Range results block headers.
Inner Macro for API. Not for user, only for development.
Inner values for API. Max buffer size depends of the selected output.

memberdef.name: VL53L8CX_MAX_RESULTS_SIZE
memberdef.initializer: ( 40U \ + L5CX_AMB_SIZE + L5CX_SPAD_SIZE + L5CX_NTAR_SIZE + L5CX_SPS_SIZE \ + L5CX_SIGR_SIZE + L5CX_DIST_SIZE + L5CX_RFLEST_SIZE + L5CX_STA_SIZE \ + L5CX_MOT_SIZE + 20U)

briefdescription.para: Macro VL53L8CX_MAX_RESULTS_SIZE indicates the maximum size used by output through I2C. Value 40 corresponds to headers + meta-data + common-data and 20 corresponds to the footer.
Macro VL53L8CX_TEMPORARY_BUFFER_SIZE can be used to know the size of the temporary buffer. The minimum size is 1024, and the maximum depends of the output configuration.
Mandatory function used to initialize the sensor. This function must be called after a power on, to load the firmware into the VL53L8CX. It takes a few hundred milliseconds.

simplesect.para: (uint8_t) status : 0 if initialization is OK.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to change the I2C address of the sensor. If multiple VL53L5 sensors are connected to the same I2C line, all other LPn pins needs to be set to Low. The default sensor address is 0x52.

simplesect.para: (uint8_t) status : 0 if new address is OK

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the current sensor power mode.

parameterdescription.para: *p_power_mode : Current power mode. The value of this pointer is equal to 0 if the sensor is in low power, (VL53L8CX_POWER_MODE_SLEEP), or 1 if sensor is in standard mode (VL53L8CX_POWER_MODE_WAKEUP).

simplesect.para: (uint8_t) status : 0 if power mode is OK

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set the sensor in Low Power mode, for example if the sensor is not used during a long time. The macro VL53L8CX_POWER_MODE_SLEEP can be used to enable the low power mode. When user want to restart the sensor, he can use macro VL53L8CX_POWER_MODE_WAKEUP. Please ensure that the device is not streaming before calling the function.

parameterdescription.para: power_mode : Selected power mode (VL53L8CX_POWER_MODE_SLEEP or VL53L8CX_POWER_MODE_WAKEUP)

simplesect.para: (uint8_t) status : 0 if power mode is OK, or 127 if power mode requested by user is not valid.

briefdescription.para: This function starts a ranging session. When the sensor streams, host cannot change settings 'on-the-fly'.

simplesect.para: (uint8_t) status : 0 if start is OK.

briefdescription.para: This function stops the ranging session. It must be used when the sensor streams, after calling vl53l8cx_start_ranging().

simplesect.para: (uint8_t) status : 0 if stop is OK

briefdescription.para: This function checks if a new data is ready by polling I2C. If a new data is ready, a flag will be raised.

parameterdescription.para: *p_isReady : Value of this pointer be updated to 0 if data is not ready, or 1 if a new data is ready.

simplesect.para: (uint8_t) status : 0 if I2C reading is OK

briefdescription.para: This function gets the ranging data, using the selected output and the resolution.

simplesect.para: (uint8_t) status : 0 data are successfully get.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the current resolution (4x4 or 8x8).

parameterdescription.para: *p_resolution : Value of this pointer will be equal to 16 for 4x4 mode, and 64 for 8x8 mode.

simplesect.para: (uint8_t) status : 0 if resolution is OK.

briefdescription.para: This function sets a new resolution (4x4 or 8x8).

parameterdescription.para: resolution : Use macro VL53L8CX_RESOLUTION_4X4 or VL53L8CX_RESOLUTION_8X8 to set the resolution.

simplesect.para: (uint8_t) status : 0 if set resolution is OK.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the current ranging frequency in Hz. Ranging frequency corresponds to the time between each measurement.

parameterdescription.para: *p_frequency_hz: Contains the ranging frequency in Hz.

simplesect.para: (uint8_t) status : 0 if ranging frequency is OK.

briefdescription.para: This function sets a new ranging frequency in Hz. Ranging frequency corresponds to the measurements frequency. This setting depends of the resolution, so please select your resolution before using this function.

parameterdescription.para:
frequency_hz : Contains the ranging frequency in Hz.
For 4x4, min and max allowed values are : [1;60]
For 8x8, min and max allowed values are : [1;15]

simplesect.para: (uint8_t) status : 0 if ranging frequency is OK, or 127 if the value is not correct.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the current integration time in ms.

simplesect.para: (uint8_t) status : 0 if integration time is OK.

briefdescription.para: This function sets a new integration time in ms. Integration time must be computed to be lower than the ranging period, for a selected resolution. Please note that this function has no impact on ranging mode continous.

parameterdescription.para: time_ms : Contains the integration time in ms. For all resolutions and frequency, the minimum value is 2ms, and the maximum is 1000ms.

simplesect.para: (uint8_t) status : 0 if set integration time is OK.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the current sharpener in percent. Sharpener can be changed to blur more or less zones depending of the application.

simplesect.para: (uint8_t) status : 0 if get sharpener is OK.

briefdescription.para: This function sets a new sharpener value in percent. Sharpener can be changed to blur more or less zones depending of the application. Min value is 0 (disabled), and max is 99.

simplesect.para: (uint8_t) status : 0 if set sharpener is OK.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the current target order (closest or strongest).

simplesect.para: (uint8_t) status : 0 if get target order is OK.

briefdescription.para: This function sets a new target order. Please use macros VL53L8CX_TARGET_ORDER_STRONGEST and VL53L8CX_TARGET_ORDER_CLOSEST to define the new output order. By default, the sensor is configured with the strongest output.

simplesect.para: (uint8_t) status : 0 if set target order is OK, or 127 if target order is unknown.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the ranging mode. Two modes are available using ULD : Continuous and autonomous. The default mode is Autonomous.

simplesect.para: (uint8_t) status : 0 if get ranging mode is OK.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set the ranging mode. Two modes are available using ULD : Continuous and autonomous. The default mode is Autonomous.

simplesect.para: (uint8_t) status : 0 if set ranging mode is OK.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to check if the synchronization pin is enabled or disabled. When it is enabled, the sensor waits an interrupt on B1 pin to start the next measurement. It is useful for multi-devices synchronization. By default the sync pin is disabled.

parameterdescription.para: p_is_sync_pin_enabled : Pointer of sync pin status. Value overridden to 0 if the pin is disabled, or 1 if the pin is enabled.

simplesect.para: (uint8_t) status : 0 if get sync pin OK.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to enable or disable the synchronization pin. When it is enabled, the sensor waits an interrupt on B1 pin to start the next measurement. It is useful for multi-devices synchronization. By default the sync pin is disabled.

parameterdescription.para: enable_sync_pin : Ste the value to 1 to enable the sync pin, or 0 to disable it.

simplesect.para: (uint8_t) status : 0 if set sync pin OK.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the number of frames between 2 temperature compensation.

parameterdescription.para: *p_repeat_count : Number of frames before next temperature compensation. Set to 0 to disable the feature (default configuration).

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set a periodic temperature compensation. By setting a repeat count different to 0 the firmware automatically runs a temperature calibration every N frames. default the repeat count is set to 0.

parameterdescription.para: repeat_count : Number of frames between temperature compensation. Set to 0 to disable the feature (default configuration).

briefdescription.para: This function can be used to read 'extra data' from DCI. Using a known index, the function fills the casted structure passed in argument.

parameterdescription.para: *data : This field can be a casted structure, or a simple array. Please note that the FW only accept data of 32 bits. So field data can only have a size of 32, 64, 96, 128, bits ....
: This field must be the structure or array size (using sizeof() function).

simplesect.para: (uint8_t) status : 0 if OK

briefdescription.para: This function can be used to write 'extra data' to DCI. The data can be simple data, or casted structure.

parameterdescription.para: *data : This field can be a casted structure, or a simple array. Please note that the FW only accept data of 32 bits. So field data can only have a size of 32, 64, 96, 128, bits ..
: This field must be the structure or array size (using sizeof() function).

simplesect.para: (uint8_t) status : 0 if OK

memberdef.argsstring: (VL53L8CX_Configuration *p_dev, uint8_t *data, uint32_t index, uint16_t data_size, uint8_t *new_data, uint16_t new_data_size, uint16_t new_data_pos)
memberdef.name: vl53l8cx_dci_replace_data

briefdescription.para: This function can be used to replace 'extra data' in DCI. The data can be simple data, or casted structure.

parameterdescription.para: *data : This field can be a casted structure, or a simple array. Please note that the FW only accept data of 32 bits. So field data can only have a size of 32, 64, 96, 128, bits ..
: This field must be the structure or array size (using sizeof() function).
new_data_pos : New data position into the buffer.

simplesect.para: (uint8_t) status : 0 if OK
